arguments.title: Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets
arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into agent-facing targets.

Three reference scenarios:
1. Viewer link — deep-link to the domain viewer server (ticket-viewer, spec-viewer, doc-viewer, log-viewer) opening the referenced entity.
2. Manifest path — relative path to the entity's manifest file (e.g. ticket.toml, spec manifest).
3. Description path — relative path to the entity's top description/body file.

Requirements:
- A single canonical source (recurring cross-cutting principles spec section) so the format can be switched in one place.
- A first-class AGENTS.md section so every agent/prompt that reads AGENTS.md inherits it.
- Path normalization: always emit forward-slash (unix) relative paths from the repo root; assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL environment; never emit drive-letter Windows paths or backslashes.
- Regenerate AGENTS.md via rule sync-targets.

arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Implementation
- Canonical source of truth: new `clickable-references` section in the recurring cross-cutting principles spec ([.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md)); spec body Sections list + related tickets updated.
- New canonical AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (`.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638`), wired as a node in [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml).
- Regenerated [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) (new "Clickable Reference Policy" section) and the rule catalog (`.rule/README.md`, `.rule/index.toon`, `.agents/rules-catalog.md`).

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by a single switch (default `manifest`): `viewer` (viewer-server deep link — ticket-viewer :3002, spec-viewer :4002, doc-viewer :3001, log-viewer :3000), `manifest` (relative link to entity manifest), `description` (relative link to top description/body file). Link text `<short-id> <title>`. Path normalization: forward-slash unix paths, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption, no backticks around file references.

## Validation
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0 (AGENTS.md byte-stable).
- `rule store-index` regenerated catalog artifacts cleanly (exit 0).
- Note: aggregated `rule sync-targets` reports pre-existing drift in the viewer-api submodule spec store (798c9a3c body.md) unrelated to this change; not touched. AGENTS.md was written via the scoped `generate-target`.

## Docs
AGENTS.md is the global render surface; copilot-instructions.md and all agents/prompts defer to it. Follow-up (optional): propagate the same policy into per-domain prompt/instruction targets (ticket/spec prompts) so their local link forms defer to this global switch.

turns.content:
Done. I added one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and rendered it into the global agent contract.

## What changed

- **Canonical source of truth** — new `clickable-references` section in the recurring cross-cutting principles spec: [.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md). This is the single place to switch reference format.
- **Global render surface** — new AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` wired as a node in [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml); [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#L167) now carries a "Clickable Reference Policy" section that every agent/prompt (and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, which defers to AGENTS.md) inherits.
- Regenerated the rule catalog (`.rule/README.md`, `.rule/index.toon`, `.agents/rules-catalog.md`).
- Tracking ticket: [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml) (now `in-review`).

## The policy

Three reference forms selected by one switch (default `manifest`):
1. `viewer` — deep link to the domain viewer server (ticket-viewer `:3002/workspace/<ws>/ticket/<id>`, spec-viewer `:4002/specs/<id>`, doc-viewer `:3001`, log-viewer `:3000`).
2. `manifest` — relative link to the entity manifest file.
3. `description` — relative link to the entity's top description/body file.

Path normalization: always forward-slash unix paths, repo-root-relative, never drive-letter absolutes, assuming a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, and no backticks around file references.

## Validation
- `rule generate-target … --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0 (AGENTS.md byte-stable).
- `rule store-index` → exit 0.

Two notes: the aggregated `rule sync-targets` reports **pre-existing** drift in the viewer-api submodule spec store (unrelated to this change — confirmed by stashing my edits), so I wrote AGENTS.md via the scoped `generate-target`. Unrelated `memory-api` submodule ticket churn is background activity and was left untouched.

Optional follow-up: make the per-domain prompt/instruction link forms (ticket/spec prompts) explicitly defer to this global switch rather than restating their own `ticket.toml` form.

I read the actual files. The self-review reads like a press release; the code tells a funnier story. Verdict below, worst crime first.

## 💀 Crime #1 — The policy violates itself on the same line it's written

Your shiny new rule body (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md#L15) and its AGENTS.md twin (AGENTS.md#L182) contain this instruction:

> "Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks."

Immediately followed, in the *same document*, by:

> "a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`" … "for example a spec's body.md" … "drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`"

That's four backtick-wrapped path references inside the rule that bans backtick-wrapped path references. You wrote a law and got arrested by it before the ink dried. This isn't a policy, it's a philosophical paradox with YAML wiring. Chef's kiss.

## 🔥 Crime #2 — "Single global switch" ships as *two* switches in one file

You sold this as "the single place to switch reference format." Cute. Except AGENTS.md#L149 already has a **"Formatting conflict policy (canonical)"** — the word *canonical* is literally in it — telling agents to use forward-slash markdown links, no backslashes, no backticks. Then 40 lines later at AGENTS.md#L177 your **"Clickable Reference Policy"** shows up saying... forward-slash paths, no backslashes, no backticks. Two canonical policies, same file, overlapping mandate, zero cross-reference between them.

The best part? Your own session reasoning (.session/sessions/5101cf18-4ca1-4298-879d-ab4831a23a5e/events.json#L3105) says:

> "rule 7a21f7ef in AGENTS.md, so that's the natural place for a global clickable-references policy... expand"

You *identified the correct home*, then built a duplicate wing next door anyway. That's not architecture, that's a timeshare. Now every agent gets to enjoy resolving which "canonical" policy wins.

## 🗑️ Crime #3 — The "source of truth" is a museum exhibit

The ticket brags about the canonical spec section clickable-references.md as "the single place to switch." Here's the twist: AGENTS.md is generated from the **rule entry** via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml#L42 (`section: agent-rules/clickable-references`), not from the spec. Nothing in the generation pipeline reads that spec section.

So you have two near-verbatim copies of the same prose — the spec (decorative, canonical-in-name-only) and the rule body (the one that actually renders) — that a human must now keep in sync **by hand**. You built a "single source of truth" that is actually two sources requiring manual synchronization. The spec is a beautifully framed portrait of a switch that isn't wired to anything.

## ⚠️ Crime #4 — Half the reference modes are `// TODO: figure it out`

The `viewer` mode gives crisp routes for tickets (`/ticket/<id>`) and specs (`/specs/<id>`), then completely gives up:

- doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
- log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`

`<doc-route>` and `<log-route>` are undefined incantations. You defined a "format-switchable policy" where switching to `viewer` for half your entity types dumps the agent at "good luck, construct a URL from vibes." Two of four viewers documented, two waved through with placeholder hand-jazz.

## 🎭 Crime #5 — Validation theater with a standing ovation

Your victory laps:
- `generate-target --check → exit 0 (byte-stable)` — congratulations, you proved the file you just wrote equals the file you just wrote. That's not a test, it's a mirror.
- Meanwhile the aggregated `rule sync-targets` gate is **red** (drift in viewer-api), so you routed around the failing check with a narrower scoped command and declared green.

Maybe the drift is genuinely pre-existing and unrelated — plausible. But "the full gate fails so I ran a smaller gate that passes" is exactly the sentence every incident postmortem quotes in the timeline section.

---

## Verdict

Technically competent plumbing wrapped around a genuinely muddled design. The rule renders, the YAML is wired, the ticket is tidy. But the actual *content* is a self-contradicting, duplicated, half-specified policy whose "single source of truth" doesn't feed the thing it claims to control — validated by a check that can only ever pass. It's a Rube Goldberg machine for the sentence "use forward slashes and don't use backticks."

**Highest-leverage fix:** Kill the duplicate. Fold this into the existing canonical `Formatting conflict policy` at 7a21f7ef (your own reasoning already picked it), make AGENTS.md generate from **one** entry, delete the decorative spec-copy or actually wire generation to it, define `<doc-route>`/`<log-route>`, and — for the love of monospace — either scrub the backticks out of the anti-backtick rule or admit the rule means "in agent responses, not in the policy prose" and *say so*. Right now it reads as "do as I say, not as I'm literally typed."
You're right on all five. Let me fix them properly rather than defend. First, let me get the real doc-viewer and log-viewer routes so `viewer` mode isn't hand-jazz.

arguments.oldString:
## Clickable Reference Policy

Render every reference to a workspace entity (ticket, spec, doc, log, or one of their files) as a clickable markdown link in **all** responses. This policy is the single global switch: change the reference format here and it applies everywhere agents and prompts emit references. Canonical source: the `clickable-references` section of the recurring cross-cutting principles spec.

Emit a reference in exactly one of three forms, selected by the active reference mode (`manifest` is the default):

1. `viewer` — a deep link to the domain viewer server that opens the entity:
   - ticket-viewer: `http://localhost:3002/workspace/<workspace>/ticket/<id>`
   - spec-viewer: `http://localhost:4002/specs/<id>`
   - doc-viewer: `http://localhost:3001/<doc-route>`
   - log-viewer: `http://localhost:3000/<log-route>`
2. `manifest` — a relative link to the entity's manifest file (for example a ticket's `ticket.toml` or a spec's `spec.toml`).
3. `description` — a relative link to the entity's top description/body file (for example a spec's `body.md`).

Link text is always `<short-id> <title>`, where `<short-id>` is the first 8 characters of the authoritative entity id and `<title>` is the authoritative entity title.

Path normalization:
- Always emit forward-slash (unix) paths; convert any Windows backslashes.
- Emit repo-root-relative paths; never emit drive-letter absolute paths such as `C:/...`.
- Assume a mingw (Git Bash) or WSL shell, so a repo-relative unix path resolves for both file links and terminal use.
- Never wrap a clickable file or path reference in backticks.

Resolve manifest and description paths from the owning API (ticket-api, spec-api, …), not from a template. If the first response omits the folder path, run a follow-up call (for example `ticket get <id> --json` and read `.payload.ticket.path`) before composing the reference.

arguments.body:
# Recurring cross-cutting principles

This spec captures cross-cutting design principles that appear repeatedly across context-engine workspace specs and agent guidance. Each principle has its own section so it can be referenced individually from generated documents and so a `rule scan` produces one canonical rule entry per principle.

The four principles owned at the context-engine root apply across every nested workspace (memory-api, viewer-api, doc/log/ticket/spec viewers, audit, context-trace/search/read/insert/api).

## Sections

- `browser-validation` — Mandatory external-browser verification for any change to a server interface or frontend feature.
- `generated-file-markers` — `*-api:file` and `*-api:entry` provenance markers and byte-stable regeneration.
- `traceability-link-format` — Canonical `[<short-id> <title>](<canonical folder>/ticket.toml)` link form for ticket references in chat output and docs.
- `validation-evidence` — Specs must link related tickets, updated docs, and passing or blocked validation results before any ticket reaches `in-review`.

## Related tickets

- [f147eb0e Migrate recurring spec principles to canonical rule entries via spec sync-generated](.ticket/tickets/f147eb0e-c758-459b-a956-a1162c3e1af6/ticket.toml)
- [a5fe4c58 Adopt rule targets for generated spec artifacts](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a5fe4c58-f59c-4d97-8ee6-3447724b5fac/ticket.toml)

## Related specs

- `spec-api/generated-documents` (`1cf68c36-7f64-4d81-b553-1947b978fbe3` in memory-viewers/memory-api)

arguments.description:
Define one global, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and render it into the global agent contract.

## Design (revised after review)
Single canonical owner: the AGENTS.md rule entry `agent-rules/clickable-references` (.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-464a...). There is no second copy:
- The "Formatting conflict policy (canonical)" block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates reference-formatting rules — it now defers to the Clickable Reference Policy. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways.
- The earlier decorative recurring-principles spec section was deleted (spec 954d9807 section clickable-references removed; body reverted). Generation reads the rule entry via rule-targets/10-agents.yaml, not the spec, so a spec copy would have required manual sync for no benefit. The rule entry is the source of truth.

## Policy contents
Three reference modes selected by one switch (default manifest):
- viewer — real routes only: ticket-viewer /workspace/{workspace}/ticket/{id} (:3002), spec-viewer /specs/{id} (:4002), log-viewer /#/file/{url-encoded-log-name} (:3000). doc-viewer (:3001) has no per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by package::target); docs fall back to manifest/description mode — stated explicitly instead of a placeholder.
- manifest — relative link to the entity manifest (ticket.toml, spec.toml).
- description — relative link to the top description/body file (body.md, description.md).
Link text "{short-id} {title}". Path normalization: forward-slash unix, repo-root-relative, no drive letters, mingw/WSL assumption. Anti-backtick rule is explicitly scoped to the emitted reference token (not policy prose or shell commands), and the policy prose no longer backtick-wraps path examples — the rule no longer violates itself.

## Validation (honest)
- `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` → exit 0. This proves the AGENTS.md render is deterministic/byte-stable for this target; it is NOT an end-to-end gate.
- `rule store-index` → exit 0 (catalog regenerated: .rule/README.md, .rule/index.toon, .agents/rules-catalog.md).
- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` remains RED, but only on a viewer-api submodule spec artifact (798c9a3c body.md) that is untouched by this change — confirmed by stashing this change and re-running the gate (still red on the same file). This change is not committed through the aggregated writer; the viewer-api drift is a separate, pre-existing problem to fix in that submodule.

## Files
.rule/rules/ce05ee88.../body.md (policy), .rule/rules/7a21f7ef.../body.md (deferral), rule-targets/10-agents.yaml (node), AGENTS.md (regenerated), .spec/specs/954d9807.../ (section deleted, body reverted), .rule/README.md + index.toon + .agents/rules-catalog.md (catalog).

turns.content:
All five addressed. Here's what changed and why.

**Crime #1 — self-violating anti-backtick rule.** The policy prose no longer backtick-wraps any path token (`ticket.toml`, `spec.toml`, `body.md`, `C:/...` are now plain text). I also added an explicit **Scope** clause: the anti-backtick rule governs the *emitted reference token*, not illustrative prose or backticked shell commands. The rule no longer arrests itself.

**Crime #2 — two "canonical" policies.** Killed the duplicate mandate. The `Formatting conflict policy (canonical)` block in the Instruction Precedence entry (7a21f7ef) no longer restates the rules — it now says references are *owned by* the Clickable Reference Policy and to not restate them. The Clickable Reference Policy back-references it. One definition, cross-linked both ways, zero overlapping mandate.

**Crime #3 — decorative "source of truth."** Deleted the spec section [.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/sections/clickable-references.md](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d) and reverted the spec body. Generation reads the rule entry via [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](rule-targets/10-agents.yaml), so the spec copy was manual-sync debt for no benefit. The rule entry is now the sole source of truth — no framed portrait of an unwired switch.

**Crime #4 — placeholder routes.** Replaced `<doc-route>`/`<log-route>` with the real ones I traced in the frontends: log-viewer `http://localhost:3000/#/file/{url-encoded-log-name}` (from [app.rs](tools/viewer/log-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/app.rs#L197) `parse_route`/`build_route`). doc-viewer has **no** per-entity deep-link route (artifacts keyed by `package::target`, no URL routing) — so instead of hand-jazz, the policy says docs fall back to manifest/description mode until a route exists.

**Crime #5 — validation theater.** The ticket now states plainly that `generate-target --check` proves only that the AGENTS.md render is deterministic (a mirror, not an end-to-end gate), and that the aggregated `rule sync-targets --check` is **red** on an untouched viewer-api submodule artifact — verified by stashing this change and re-running (still red on the same file). No green declared where the full gate is red.

Verified: AGENTS.md byte-stable (`generate-target … --check` exit 0), catalog regenerated (exit 0). Footprint: two rule bodies, the YAML node (already committed), regenerated AGENTS.md, spec section deletion + body revert, and catalog files.
